description: Unit: quota resolver uses an active Stripe subscription current_period_start/current_period_end and stores period_source stripe_subscription.
Unit: quota resolver falls back to the current UTC calendar month and tier defaults when no valid active Stripe period exists.
Unit: quota resolver applies metadata precedence of Stripe price metadata, then product metadata, then tier default.
Unit: quota resolver maps workflow_step_limit=unlimited to effective_limit null and ignores invalid metadata by falling back safely.
DB integration: migrated workflow_step_usage_periods supports insert/upsert by tenant, period_start, and period_end and enforces uniqueness.
DB integration: atomic reservation increments used_count below a finite limit and rejects without incrementing at the limit.
DB integration: concurrent finite reservations cannot increase used_count beyond effective_limit.
DB integration: unlimited reservations always succeed and still increment used_count for reporting.
Runtime integration: DB WorkflowRuntimeV2 reserves quota before creating a STARTED step row and executes normally under the limit.
Runtime integration: DB WorkflowRuntimeV2 quota exhaustion creates a quota wait, marks the run WAITING at the current node_path, and creates no step row for the blocked step.
Runtime integration: retry attempts and forEach body attempts each consume additional quota when the attempt starts.
Runtime integration: event.wait, time.wait, or human.task consumes quota on first entry and does not repeatedly consume while the run remains waiting.
Temporal integration: projectWorkflowRuntimeV2StepStart reserves quota before creating a STARTED step row and reports quota exhaustion as a controlled pause/wait path.
Job integration: workflow-quota-resume-scan skips exhausted tenants, resolves eligible quota waits, and sets matching runs back to RUNNING without pre-consuming quota.
Job integration: concurrent quota resume scans use row locking or equivalent safeguards so the same wait is not resolved twice.
Action/API integration: manual quota resume verifies tenant ownership and permissions, resumes when quota is available, and does not bypass runtime reservation.
Action/API integration: manual quota resume returns used count, effective limit, and reset time when quota remains exhausted.
Diagnostic integration: reconciliation reports drift between workflow_step_usage_periods.used_count and workflow_run_steps joined to workflow_runs for a tenant period.
Observability: quota reservation, quota exceeded, resume, invalid metadata fallback, and fallback-calendar usage emit structured logs or workflow run logs with tenant and period context.
Documentation review: support and engineering notes describe quota source, default limits, metadata override, pause/resume behavior, and reconciliation procedure.
